question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: CommonConfig] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CommonConfig.Realm` or `CommonConfig.Shard` fields being set to unexpected values (e.g., negative or very large integers) to cause `NewAccountIdFromPublicKeyBytes` to generate account IDs with incorrect shard/realm, causing fee payments to be directed to non-existent or attacker-controlled accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: CommonConfig] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `CommonConfig.Realm` and `CommonConfig.Shard` both being zero (default) to cause the rosetta node to generate account IDs in shard 0 realm 0 regardless of the actual network configuration, causing fee-related account lookups to resolve incorrectly and fees to be routed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Feature] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Feature.SubNetworkIdentifier` boolean being toggled to cause the rosetta node to include or exclude sub-network identifiers in fee-related responses, causing fee calculations to be applied to the wrong sub-network and resulting in fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Log] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Log.Level` field being set to `debug` or `trace` to cause the rosetta node to log sensitive fee-related configuration data (including `Db.Password` before it is masked), enabling credential theft that allows the attacker to directly manipulate fee schedule data in the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.Port` field being set to a privileged port number (below 1024) to cause the rosetta node to fail to bind, preventing fee metadata from being served and causing clients to use incorrect default fees?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `NodeMap` with duplicate IP:port keys but different `hiero.AccountID` values, causing non-deterministic node selection in `getRandomNodeAccountId()` and resulting in transactions being submitted to nodes with different fee structures, modifying effective fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user supply an empty `NodeMap` (zero nodes) to cause `getRandomNodeAccountId()` to fail, preventing `ConstructionMetadata` from returning a valid node account ID and blocking all transaction construction, effectively making fee metadata unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.Network` field accepting arbitrary lowercase strings (after `strings.ToLower`) to supply a network name that resolves to an unexpected SDK client configuration with different fee schedules, causing `GetDefaultMaxTransactionFee()` to return fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Db.Port` field being a `uint16` with no upper-bound validation beyond type constraints to supply port 0, causing the database connection to fail and fee schedule data to be unavailable, forcing the node to use hardcoded default fees?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Db.Host` field accepting an arbitrary string (including IP addresses, hostnames, or Unix socket paths) to redirect database connections to an attacker-controlled host that returns manipulated fee schedule data, causing transaction fees to be calculated outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Pool] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Pool.MaxOpenConnections` value of zero (which in some Go database drivers means unlimited connections) to cause unbounded database connection creation under load, exhausting database resources and causing fee schedule queries to fail, resulting in default fees being used outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.NodeVersion` field being an arbitrary string with no validation to inject a version string that causes the SDK to select a different protocol version with different fee encoding, resulting in fee values being misinterpreted outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeMapDecodeHookFunc` accepting any `map[string]interface{}` input to inject a node entry with a malformed account ID string that passes initial parsing but resolves to an unexpected `hiero.AccountID`, causing node fees to be directed to an unintended account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config` struct's YAML deserialization (via viper) to inject unexpected fields that override fee-related configuration values through environment variable overrides (via `AutomaticEnv`), causing fee parameters to be set outside design constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Db.Name` field to supply a database name pointing to a different schema that lacks fee schedule tables, causing fee-related queries to return empty results and the node to use default hardcoded fees outside current network parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Http] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Http.WriteTimeout` shorter than the time required to complete a fee schedule database query, causing the `/construction/metadata` response to be truncated and the client to receive a partial or zero `SuggestedFee` value?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.ShutdownTimeout` being set to a very large value to prevent the rosetta node from restarting after a configuration update that corrects fee parameters, causing the node to continue serving incorrect fees for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `loadNodeMapFromEnv()` function's parsing of the `HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES` environment variable by injecting a node string with extra colons (e.g., `1.2.3.4:50211:0.0.3:extra`) that causes `parts[2]` to be an unexpected value, resulting in an incorrect node account ID being used for fee routing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `loadNodeMapFromEnv()` function's use of `strings.Split(node, \

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the viper `AutomaticEnv()` configuration loading to override `Config.Nodes` via environment variables using the `HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES` key, injecting attacker-controlled node account IDs that redirect node fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Pool] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Pool.MaxLifetime` value set to a very short duration (e.g., 1 millisecond) to cause constant database connection recycling during fee schedule queries, creating race conditions where fee data is read from a partially-initialized connection and incorrect fees are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Db.Username` field to supply a database username with elevated privileges (if misconfigured), allowing the rosetta node to execute fee schedule modifications that would otherwise be restricted to a read-only user?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.Online` field being `false` to cause the rosetta node to skip live network fee schedule validation, allowing transactions to be constructed with arbitrarily low max fees that would be rejected by the network but are accepted by the offline rosetta node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Response] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock` field being set to `int` (signed) with no minimum validation to supply a negative value, causing the rosetta node to behave unexpectedly when slicing transaction arrays and potentially omitting fee operations from block responses?
